lesson: Stratifying product and a sluggish agitator with no alarm point to a glazed, slipping drive belt. The motor is usually perfectly healthy.
symptoms: Product stratifying — ice sinking, syrup floating.

tests.inspect-beater: The drive side looks tired — belt glazed shiny, carbon dust around the motor vents. The motor spins; the auger doesn't always follow.

symptoms: Slush separating into layers within the hour.
Squeal from the drive on startup, then silence.
One bowl mixing noticeably worse than the other.

flavour: A five-dollar belt was standing between this syrup and a uniform mixture.
authenticityNote: Slushie agitator drive belts glaze and slip with age, causing stratification before they fail completely. The motor is usually perfectly healthy.
